commit.message:
Make maintenance table rows clickable to navigate to record detail (#144)

* Initial plan

* Make maintenance table rows clickable to navigate to record detail page

Add data-href attribute and table-row-link class to maintenance table rows
on dashboard, maintenance list, and vehicle detail pages. Add JavaScript
handler (initClickableRows) that navigates to the URL on row click while
preserving clicks on interactive elements (links, buttons). Add cursor:
pointer CSS for clickable rows. Include maintenance record ID in dashboard
handler data. Update minified CSS and JS.

Co-authored-by: truggeri <28942816+truggeri@users.noreply.github.com>

* Re-implement clickable rows with CSS-only approach (no JavaScript)

Replace the JavaScript initClickableRows() approach with the CSS-only
technique from stackoverflow.com/a/8807514: wrap each td content in an
<a> tag styled as display:block to fill the cell. Padding is transferred
from td to the inner <a> so the entire cell area is clickable. The
:not(.btn) selector preserves existing button styling in the Actions
column. Mobile responsive rules reset the <a> to inline display.

Co-authored-by: truggeri <28942816+truggeri@users.noreply.github.com>

* Adding minified assets

commit.message:
test(incremental-parsing): add comprehensive unit tests (#1107)

Add 90 new unit tests to perl-incremental-parsing covering:
- LineIndex: unicode, CRLF, multi-blank-line, boundary positions
- IncrementalState: source preservation, rope, checkpoints, edge cases
- Edit/IncrementalEdit: shifts, overlaps, boundaries, LSP conversion
- IncrementalEditSet: sort, reverse, apply, shift arithmetic
- apply_edits: insert/delete/replace at various positions
- IncrementalDocument: version tracking, source update, metrics, cache
- SubtreeCache: population after parse, LRU tracking
- SimpleIncrementalParser: multiple edits, empty source, no-edit reparse
- CheckpointedIncrementalParser: stats, clear/reparse, edit preservation
- IncrementalParserV2: metrics, config, sequential edits, structural fallback
- IncrementalTree: containing node lookup, out-of-range queries
- IncrementalMetrics: all performance categories, boundary conditions
- AdvancedReuseAnalyzer: stats, config, analysis result formatting
- DocumentParser: full/incremental modes, content/metrics accessors
- Edge cases: empty/single-char/large documents, scope snapshots

Total: 162 tests (72 existing + 90 new), all passing.

commit.message:
test(lexer): add comprehensive unit tests (#1106)

Add 133 unit tests for the perl-lexer crate covering:

- Token construction, properties (len, is_empty), and type equality
- LexerMode variants (ExpectTerm, ExpectOperator, etc.)
- LexerError types and position extraction
- LexerConfig defaults and custom configuration
- Lexer creation (new, with_config, with_body_tokens)
- collect_tokens, peek_token, reset, next_token lifecycle
- set_mode and enter_format_mode
- Keyword, variable/sigil, and numeric literal tokenization
- String literals (single, double, backtick, empty, escapes)
- Operator disambiguation (arithmetic, comparison, assignment, etc.)
- Context-sensitive slash/percent disambiguation (division vs regex)
- Quote-like operators (q, qq, qw, qr, qx, m, s, tr, y)
- Heredoc tokenization and body token emission
- Data sections (__DATA__, __END__) and POD handling
- UTF-8 BOM skipping
- Checkpoint save/restore, cache, diff, edit application
- Edge cases: empty input, unicode, CJK, long identifiers, deep nesting
- Real-world Perl snippets (OO, regex, conditionals, loops)

All tests follow project standards:
- No unwrap()/expect() in test code
- Functions return () or Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(module-name): add comprehensive unit tests (#1105)

Add 47 new tests to comprehensive_unit_tests.rs (99 total) covering:
- Idempotency of normalize and legacy transforms
- Deterministic/stable variant pair output
- Real-world CPAN module names (DBI, DBD::mysql, Test::More, LWP)
- Very long module names (20 segments)
- Separator-only strings
- Asymmetric variant pairs (qualified vs bare)
- Character preservation (underscores, hyphens, digits)
- Cow lifetime and pointer identity guarantees
- Cow equality/comparison semantics
- Vector content guarantees (always 1-2 pairs)
- Mixed legacy+canonical separators in single input
- Common Perl pragmas as bare names
- Adjacent separator edge cases
- into_owned conversion
- Identity renames
- Depth mismatches between old and new names
- Unicode: CJK, emoji, accented characters
- Roundtrip with CJK characters

* feat(workspace): Add pnpm workspace configuration with ignored built dependencies

* Add best practices for React and server-side development

- Introduced guidelines for calculating derived state during rendering to avoid redundant renders.
- Added recommendations for subscribing to derived state to reduce re-render frequency.
- Emphasized the use of functional setState updates to prevent stale closures and unnecessary callback recreations.
- Suggested lazy state initialization for expensive initial values to optimize performance.
- Provided guidance on extracting default non-primitive parameter values from memoized components to restore memoization.
- Encouraged extracting expensive work into memoized components to enable early returns.
- Recommended placing interaction logic in event handlers to avoid unnecessary effect re-runs.
- Advised against wrapping simple expressions in useMemo to prevent wasted computation.
- Promoted the use of transitions for non-urgent updates to maintain UI responsiveness.
- Suggested using useRef for transient values to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
- Introduced the use of after() for non-blocking operations in server actions.
- Highlighted the importance of authenticating server actions like API routes to prevent unauthorized access.
- Implemented cross-request LRU caching for shared data across sequential requests.
- Recommended per-request deduplication with React.cache() for efficient data fetching.
- Advised avoiding duplicate serialization in RSC props to reduce network payload.
- Encouraged parallel data fetching with component composition to eliminate server-side waterfalls.
- Suggested minimizing serialization at RSC boundaries to reduce data transfer size.
- Added a new skill for reviewing UI code against Web Interface Guidelines compliance.

* Add best practices for React and server-side development

- Introduced guidelines for calculating derived state during rendering to avoid redundant renders.
- Added recommendations for subscribing to derived state to reduce re-render frequency.
- Emphasized the use of functional setState updates to prevent stale closures and unnecessary callback recreations.
- Suggested lazy state initialization for expensive initial values to optimize performance.
- Provided guidance on extracting default non-primitive parameter values from memoized components to restore memoization.
- Encouraged extracting expensive work into memoized components to enable early returns.
- Recommended placing interaction logic in event handlers to avoid unnecessary effect re-runs.
- Advised against wrapping simple expressions in useMemo to prevent wasted computation.
- Promoted the use of transitions for non-urgent updates to maintain UI responsiveness.
- Suggested using useRef for transient values to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
- Introduced the use of after() for non-blocking operations in server actions.
- Highlighted the importance of authenticating server actions like API routes to prevent unauthorized access.
- Implemented cross-request LRU caching for shared data across sequential requests.
- Recommended per-request deduplication with React.cache() for efficient data fetching.
- Advised avoiding duplicate serialization in RSC props to reduce network payload.
- Encouraged parallel data fetching with component composition to eliminate server-side waterfalls.
- Suggested minimizing serialization at RSC boundaries to reduce data transfer size.
- Added a new skill for reviewing UI code against Web Interface Guidelines compliance.

commit.message:
Enable evaluation for fork PRs via reusable workflow (#198)

* Enable evaluation for fork PRs via reusable workflow

Refactor the evaluation workflow into three files:

- evaluation-run.yml: reusable workflow with build-validator, evaluate,
  and comment-on-pr jobs (shared by both callers)
- evaluation.yml: thin caller for pull_request and schedule triggers;
  skips fork PRs which are handled by the new fork workflow
- evaluation-fork-pr.yml: thin caller using pull_request_target with
  environment-gated secrets for fork PRs

Also clear GITHUB_TOKEN from the process environment after the
CopilotClient reads it, so eval scenarios and setup commands cannot
access the credential.

commit.message:
feat(cli): add interactive setup wizard (#72)

Adds a -i / --interactive flag that launches a guided terminal wizard
for first-time users. The wizard walks through password, port, access
mode (tunnel type and visibility), and log level, then starts the server
with the chosen config.

Key changes:
- Extract prompt helpers (color, ask, choose, confirm) from service.js
into src/prompts.js so both the service install wizard and the new setup
wizard can share them
- Add src/interactive.js with the wizard flow (runs in an alternate
screen buffer)
- Port validation clamped to 1-65535, passwords masked in summary output
- Alternate screen buffer cleaned up on Ctrl+C
- Docs updated: architecture, configuration, getting-started, README,
landing page, MkDocs index

Closes #71

commit.message:
feat: add positions field to terrain_pack.v1 and resolved_map.v1 schemas (#16)

* feat: add positions field to terrain_pack.v1 and resolved_map.v1 schemas

Add optional `positions` array field to transition entries in
terrain_pack.v1 and cellClassificationEntry.transition in
resolved_map.v1 per RFC Forge#234.

- positions: alphabetically sorted string array of named spatial
  locations (model-specific enum via allOf conditionals)
- marching-squares: DL, DR, UL, UR (corner positions)
- blob-47: E, N, NE, NW, S, SE, SW, W (neighbor directions)
- Mark cornerBits description as deprecated in both schemas
- Add VOCABULARY.md §9: position naming, spatial diagrams, reader
  algorithm, field authority during migration
- Update fixtures with positions data
- Regenerate types, update golden fixture hash

Additive optional fields only — no breaking changes. cornerBits
remains required for v1; removal deferred to v2 schema.

Closes #15

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add wang positions enum constraint for completeness

Add model-conditional positions enum ["E","N","S","W"] to the
wang allOf block, consistent with MS and blob-47 blocks. Prevents
invalid position values from passing validation when model is wang.

commit.message:
docs: COMPATIBILITY.md reality pass  fix 8 overclaims (#3268)

## Summary

Reality pass on COMPATIBILITY.md. Cross-referenced every claim against
CI workflows and actual code.

### Changes (8 fixes)

1. **Python API**: 'guarantee drop-in compatibility' 'target' (Python
bindings not validated in CI)
2. **Test files**: Removed nonexistent \gguf_compatibility.rs\ reference
3. **CI platforms**: 'Linux, macOS, Windows' 'Linux only' (actual
compatibility.yml matrix)
4. **Python version**: 'Must test 3.12+' '3.10 tested' (actual CI; PyO3
ABI3-py312 is a build target, not CI coverage)
5. **AVX-512 perf**: Removed unvalidated '2x speedup' and 'thermal
throttling fallback' claims
6. **Backend auto-detection**: Corrected CUDA>Metal>Vulkan>OpenCL>CPU
chain CUDA-or-CPU (Metal/Vulkan/OpenCL not wired into auto-detection)
7. **Verified configs**: Split into 'Tested (CI)' vs 'Intended (not yet
in CI)' removed CentOS/Windows from verified
8. **llama_sampling_\***: ' Full support' ' Planned' (no FFI wrapper
exists)

### Verification

- Checked CI workflows: ci-core.yml, compatibility.yml,
apple-silicon.yml
- Checked FFI implementation: crates/bitnet-ffi/src/lib.rs
- Checked test file existence: 3 of 4 listed files exist
(gguf_compatibility.rs does not)

Docs-only change. No code changes.

**Triage class: A (MERGE NOW)**

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add server-side endpoints for aviation news and aircraft tracking, and introduce a new DeckGLMap component for map visualization.

* Update server/worldmonitor/aviation/v1/list-airport-flights.ts

The cache key for listAirportFlights excludes limit, but the upstream fetch/simulated generator uses limit to determine how many flights to return. If the first request within TTL uses a small limit, larger subsequent requests will be incorrectly capped until cache expiry. Include limit (or a normalized bucket/max) in cacheKey, or always fetch/cache a fixed max then slice per request.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update server/worldmonitor/aviation/v1/get-flight-status.ts

getFlightStatus accepts origin, but cacheKey does not include it. This can serve cached results from an origin-less query to an origin-filtered query (or vice versa). Add origin (normalized) to the cache key or apply filtering after fetch to ensure cache correctness.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: OTPK burn-on-fetch + replace_existing for stale key pool purge

Problem: devices with stale/broken OTPK pool (99 old + 50 fresh) were
serving broken keys first due to FIFO ordering (ORDER BY uploaded_at ASC).
The burn-on-fetch was already correct (DELETE + FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED),
but no mechanism existed to purge the stale pool.

Fix:
- Add replace_existing: bool = 4 to UploadPreKeysRequest proto
- upload_prekeys() core function accepts replace_existing flag
- When replace_existing=true: atomically DELETE all existing OTPKs for
  the device before inserting the new batch
- gRPC handler passes req.replace_existing to core function
- Default false = backward compatible additive upload

Usage: iOS/Android client sends replace_existing=true when uploading
after key corruption, re-registration, or forced key refresh.
This atomically wipes stale keys and replaces with a fresh pool.

The existing FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED concurrent-safety is preserved.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
